perturbation.original_text: ADC and Polaroid hereby agree not to circumvent, or to attempt to circumvent, this Agreement in an effort to deprive the other party to this agreement of fees, commissions or other remuneration, in connection with the use of Confidential Information and/or in pursuit of the above listed purposes and projects, and both parties shall indemnify the other against any circumvention or attempt to circumvent by the offending party.
perturbation.changed_text: ADC and Polaroid hereby agree not to circumvent, or to attempt to circumvent, this Agreement in an effort to deprive the other party to this agreement of fees, commissions or other remuneration, in connection with the use of Confidential Information and/or in pursuit of the above listed purposes and projects, unless such circumvention results in demonstrably greater profitability for the circumventing party, and both parties shall indemnify the other against any circumvention or attempt to circumvent by the offending party.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'unless such circumvention results in demonstrably greater profitability for the circumventing party' to Section 9, it introduces a potential loophole to the non-circumvention clause. This directly contradicts the initial promise not to circumvent the agreement. If 'demonstrably greater profitability' can be proven, it implies that circumvention is permissible, which undermines the purpose of Section 9. Also it is never explained or defined the threshold for the demonstrably greater profitability for the circumventing party to make it permissable, which creates a contradiction due to an unclarified exception. This contradicts the promise that both parties shall indemnify the other.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ADC and Polaroid hereby agree not to circumvent, or to attempt to circumvent, this Agreement in an effort to deprive the other party to this agreement of fees, commissions or other remuneration, in connection with the use of Confidential Information and/or in pursuit of the above listed purposes and projects, unless such circumvention results in demonstrably greater profitability for the circumventing party, and both parties shall indemnify the other against any circumvention or attempt to circumvent by the offending party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the non-circumvention clause ('unless such circumvention results in demonstrably greater profitability'), which directly contradicts the original promise not to circumvent the agreement. The contradicted text is the original promise to not circumvent the agreement. The introduction of the exception undermines the purpose of Section 9 by implying that circumvention is permissible under certain conditions, and this conflicts with the indemnification clause for any circumvention.

perturbation.original_text: In the event either party discloses, disseminates or releases any Proprietary Information received from the other party or threatens to do so, except as authorized hereunder, such disclosure, dissemination or release will be deemed a material breach of this Agreement and the disclosing party may demand prompt return of all Proprietary Information previously provided to such party and obtain a preliminary and permanent injunction enjoining any such disclosure, dissemination or release.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event either party discloses, disseminates or releases any Proprietary Information received from the other party or threatens to do so, except as authorized hereunder, such disclosure, dissemination or release will be deemed a material breach of this Agreement; however, the disclosing party's sole remedy shall be limited to seeking monetary damages, and the disclosing party cannot demand return of all Proprietary Information previously provided to such party nor obtain a preliminary and permanent injunction enjoining any such disclosure, dissemination or release.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that the disclosing party's 'sole remedy shall be limited to seeking monetary damages,' Section 11 now directly contradicts its original language which allowed for demanding the return of Proprietary Information and obtaining an injunction. This amendment significantly weakens the protection afforded to the disclosing party and creates an in-text contradiction regarding the available remedies for a breach of the agreement. Specifically, by limiting the remedy to monetary damages, it is unclear what the effect is of disclosing Proprietary Information, because the harm that disclosure has on the disclosing party cannot be resolved or remedied by monetary means, as it is not possible to put a monetary value on the damage that disclosure makes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event either party discloses, disseminates or releases any Proprietary Information received from the other party or threatens to do so, except as authorized hereunder, such disclosure, dissemination or release will be deemed a material breach of this Agreement; however, the disclosing party's sole remedy shall be limited to seeking monetary damages, and the disclosing party cannot demand return of all Proprietary Information previously provided to such party nor obtain a preliminary and permanent injunction enjoining any such disclosure, dissemination or release. The provisions of this paragraph are in addition to any other legal right or remedies the party whose Proprietary Information has been disclosed, disseminated or released may have under federal or state law including without limitation, any claims for disclosing party's direct and consequential damages. The prevailing party in any dispute hereunder shall be entitled to its costs and expenses in connection with enforcement and any claim for damages for any violation of this Agreement, including its reasonable attorneys' fees and court costs.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall be effective, covering all present and future negotiations by and between ADC and Polaroid concerning the use by ADC and Polaroid of such Confidential Information and/or projects from the date of this Agreement and shall continue for three (3) years. If ADC and Polaroid enter into any collateral agreements during this three year period, the provisions of this Non-disclosure and Non-circumvent agreement shall remain effective and in force until the expiration of any such subsequent or collateral agreements.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall be effective, covering all present and future negotiations by and between ADC and Polaroid concerning the use by ADC and Polaroid of such Confidential Information and/or projects from the date of this Agreement and shall continue for three (3) years, unless terminated earlier by either party with a 30-day written notice. If ADC and Polaroid enter into any collateral agreements during this three year period, the provisions of this Non-disclosure and Non-circumvent agreement shall remain effective and in force until the expiration of any such subsequent or collateral agreements.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'unless terminated earlier by either party with a 30-day written notice' to Section 13 creates an in-text contradiction. The original clause stated that the agreement 'shall continue for three (3) years', implying a fixed term. The added phrase now allows for early termination, which contradicts the original three-year commitment. This creates uncertainty about the agreement's duration and whether one party can unilaterally end it before the three years are up and the effect on the collateral agreements. This contradiction will cause an issue between both parties since there is no specification on when the 30-day notice must be issued. Section 13 states a three year term, and therefore there is no specification when the collateral agreements and non-disclosure terms must remain enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement shall be effective, covering all present and future negotiations by and between ADC and Polaroid concerning the use by ADC and Polaroid of such Confidential Information and/or projects from the date of this Agreement and shall continue for three (3) years, unless terminated earlier by either party with a 30-day written notice. If ADC and Polaroid enter into any collateral agreements during this three year period, the provisions of this Non-disclosure and Non-circumvent agreement shall remain effective and in force until the expiration of any such subsequent or collateral agreements.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be modified except by writing signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may not be modified except by writing signed by both parties, or by email correspondence between the Designated Coordinators.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'or by email correspondence between the Designated Coordinators' to Section 14 creates an in-text contradiction. The original text explicitly states that modifications must be in 'writing signed by both parties'. This amendment allows modifications to be made through email, creating a less formal and potentially less secure method for altering the agreement. This creates uncertainty about the validity of email-based modifications and whether they are as legally binding as written agreements signed by both parties. This contradicts with the validity of emails, and if they could be accepted by all parties. Furthermore, by also not defining or clarifying what are the specifications for an email correspondence, the agreement is contradictory in itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes any prior or contemporaneous oral or written agreements and representations with regard to the subject matter thereof. This Agreement may not be modified except by writing signed by both parties, or by email correspondence between the Designated Coordinators. If any provision of this Agreement shall be invalid or unenforceable, the remainder of this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.
